semanticCore: Color, hue, diversity, variety, or complexion
quranicRelevance: Diverse colors of plants, mountains, and human languages/skins (Alwan - 'mukhtalifan alwanuhu', 'wakhtilafu alsinatikum wa alwanikum').

keyDerivations.meaning: Color, hue, variety, sort, or skin complexion

figurativeOrRareUsages: بَقَرَةٌ صَفْرَاءُ فَاقِعٌ لَوْنُهَا (2:69): A yellow cow, bright is her color (pleasing to look at).
summaryEn: The root denotes color and variety, illustrating the visual beauty of yellow cows, and using 'Alwan' to praise the natural diversity of creation and languages.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الألوان والتنوع، واصفاً لون البقرة الصفراء المبهج، ومستخدماً (ألوانكم) للتدليل على عظمة الاختلاف الطبيعي.
rawText: أَلْوَانُ الأَطْعِمَةِ [Sorts, or species, of viands]. (S in art. بأج.) تَلَوَّنَ 5 It became coloured. (MA, KL.) See 1. === It became variegated, or diversified in colour. === And hence, (see تغّول,) It varied in state, or condition; it was, or became, variable therein. He assumed various forms, or appearances. === تَلَوَّنَ فُلَانٌ Such a one varied in disposition. (Msb.) لَوَّنَ 2 i. q. 🡻تَلَوَّنَ It became coloured. (M.) === It (a palm-tree) had dates which had become coloured. (T.) === لَوَّنَ فِى الكَلَامِ [He varied in speech]. (Sgh, K, voce تَمَطَّطَ.) لَوْنٌ Colour: (S, Msb, K:) distinctive quality or property: (M, K:) sort, or species: (S, K:) mood, disposition, or character. مُتَلَوِّنٌ Varying, or variable, in dispositions. === Unsteady in disposition. (K.)
